points: Metals are characteristically lustrous, malleable, ductile, and excellent electrical conductors, whereas non-metals generally lack these distinct physical traits.
Metals readily shed valence electrons to form positive ions and basic oxides, while non-metals accept electrons to form negative ions and acidic oxides.
The reactivity series systematically ranks metals by their decreasing chemical vigor, establishing their capacity to displace lesser elements from combined salt solutions.
Metals interact directly with non-metals via full electron transfer to forge strong, rigid, high-melting ionic chemical compounds.
Raw metallic elements are purified from natural crust ores utilizing thermal roasting, calcination, or electrolytic reduction directly determined by their measured reactivity.
Environmental atmospheric corrosion progressively disintegrates exposed metals, a destructive oxidation process efficiently preventable via industrial galvanisation or protective alloying.
